recent_excerpts: Black Hills InfoSec demonstrates how attackers abuse Microsoft SSPR to bypass MFA and reset credentials via social engineering. Real-world test scenario reveals detection gaps.
Adaptive Security finds 3 seconds of audio enough to clone a voice for fraud; deepfake calls tricked employees into wiring $243K in one case. No detection tool caught the attack.
Specops Software documents how helpdesk social engineering turns password reset requests into full account compromise — attackers bypass MFA and gain persistent access.
